titleStmt.title: Letter from T. Greany to Mr Hanstock, 20 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: T. Greany

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from T. Greany to Mr Hanstock refarding pension of John Murhill.

msIdentifier.repository: UCC Library Special Collections and Archives

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ESTATE OFFICE,
MUCKROSS,
KILLARNEY. 20th July 1916 Dear Mr Hanstock, Many thanks for your
letter of 5th inst, enclosing
cheq. value £3.18.0 in discharge
of Pension for Old John Murhill
from Lady Ardilaum for Quarter
ending 24th June 1916. Please find
receipt enclosed. Mrs Williams, (Mrs Ross's second
daughter) is managing the Muckross
Hotel, for her brother Jack, who
is "somewhere in France", the
Management is, unfortunately,
very light work, as there is scarcely
a Tourist coming here this season. Many thanks for your kind
inquiries about my health. I am,
thank God, keeping well. With kind regards & hoping
you & yours are in the enjoyment
of the best of good health. I am
Yours very truly TM.Greany
